title: Macrame table runner
subtitle: A flat-knotted cotton table runner in square knots and spiral half hitches
excerpt: A macramé table runner uses a flat-knotted structure rather than a suspended hanging. Cords are attached to a foundation rod or tension bar, knotted flat on a board, and finished with fringe at both ends. The runner shown here uses 5 mm cotton cord in square knots and alternates with spiral half-hitch sections for texture.
sourceNotes: Flat macramé table runners in square knots are a well-established form in contemporary macramé practice. Method follows standard UK workshop instruction.

content.text: A table runner requires a stable
to keep even tension across the full width while knotting. The

sections break up the regularity of the square knot rows and add a three-dimensional texture that shows well on a flat table surface. The runner finishes at approximately 30 x 120 cm including fringe.

items.name: 5 mm single-strand cotton macramé cord, natural
Macramé board or thick foam board

content.text: Cut 24 cords, each 500 cm long. Pin a temporary mounting rod (a wooden dowel or length of stiff card) across the top of the macramé board. Attach each cord with a lark's head knot across the rod, spacing them evenly to give a working width of 30 cm (48 working ends, spaced approximately 6 mm apart).
Step 2: Work the square knot sections

Work 4 rows of alternating square knots across the full width. The alternating pattern produces a flat, open mesh approximately 8 cm deep. Pin the work to the board after each row to keep tension even.
Step 3: Work spiral half hitch sections

After the 4 square knot rows, work a spiral half hitch section on each cord group of 4: tie 8 half knots in the same direction (right over left only) to create a spiral twist approximately 4 cm long. The spiral columns will rotate as they form; this is the correct result.

Alternate square knot sections and spiral sections until the runner reaches the desired length (approximately 90 cm knotted section for a 120 cm finished runner). Finish with 4 rows of square knots to mirror the start. Trim all cord ends to 15 cm fringe. Remove the mounting rod and repeat the fringe treatment at the top end.

items.fix: Pin the outer edge cords to the board at each row to maintain width. Work square knots with the filler cords held taut and parallel, not angled toward each other.
items.cause: Working cords pulled inward rather than kept parallel during knotting
items.problem: The runner tapers narrower from top to bottom

items.fix: Choose one direction at the start and tie all spiral sections consistently. Mark which working cord leads (right or left) before beginning the spiral section.
items.cause: Half knots tied right-over-left in some groups and left-over-right in others
items.problem: The spiral sections are all twisting in different directions
